semanticCore: Finding, meeting with, discovering, hit upon, or matching
quranicRelevance: Finding ancestors in error (Alfaw - 'alfaw aba-ahum'), and finding a master/husband ('alfaya').

keyDerivations.meaning: To find, discover, hit upon, catch in the act, or meet with

figurativeOrRareUsages: وَأَلْفَيَا سَيِّدَهَا لَدَى الْبَابِ (12:25): And they found/met her master (husband) at the door (the sudden arrival of the Aziz in Joseph's story).
discardedNoise: Traditional Arabic slang words for finding.
summaryEn: The root denotes finding/discovering (Ilafaa'), describing the sudden encounter of Joseph and Zulaykha with the minister, and pagans finding their fathers in error.
summaryAr: يدل هذا الجذر على العثور والمصادفة والوجدان (إلفاء)، مصوراً لقاء يوسف وزليخة بالوزير بغتة عند الباب (وألفيا)، وإيجاد الكفار لآبائهم ضالين.
rawText: تَلَافَاهُ 6 i. q. تَدَارَكَهُ [He repaired it; a meaning well known]; (S, Msb, K, TA;) namely, a short-coming; or failing, or falling short, of what was requisite, or due; and an inconsistent act. (TA.) You say, هٰذَا أَمْرٌ لَا يُتَلَافَى [This is an affair that will not be repaired]. (TA.) And تَلَافَى أَمْرَهُمْ بِالصُّلْحِ [He repaired their condition by peace, or reconciliation]. (EM, p. 117.) See دَقَّهُ لَفَاةٌ : see لفَاتٌ.
